blockers: task tool did not expose background agent ids in returned output, so launch_started events were recorded without agent_id fields
consensus: recovery and teardown behavior are materially weaker than the documented/expected metaswarm guardrails
stuck detection is prone to false positives and does not safely recover partial dead-worker cases while other agents remain alive
failure containment and banned-model guardrails exist, but operational recovery coverage and documentation are incomplete
conflicts: README claims dead-agent PID recovery and strong observability, but monitor/launcher logic and smoke coverage leave notable stuck/restart and child-visibility gaps

key_findings.summary: Monitor uses claimed-file mtime >120s as the primary stuck heuristic, ignores heartbeats/progress, only requeues when all agents are dead, and can loop indefinitely if all agents die before any result.
Launcher teardown is best-effort only, restart logic can strand claimed work, and ordinary worker failure lacks symmetric state/result recovery markers.
Smoke tests are happy-path only and do not exercise stuck detection, worker death, claimed-task recovery, partial writes, or metaswarm recovery from partial child failure.
Docs describe dead-agent recovery and metaswarm partial guardrails, but underdocument stuck-yet-alive recovery, metaswarm child visibility, and teardown semantics for in-flight work.
Depth Guard, bounded spawning, partial/failed contracts, and banned-model enforcement exist, but shell-level stuck-launch timeouts are weak and a no-gates override can remove local circuit breakers.
